stem:
A computer uses 32-bit virtual address, and 32-bit physical address. The physical memory is byte addressable, and the page size is 4 Kbytes. It is decided to use two level page tables to translate from virtual to physical address. Equal number of bits should be used for indexing the first level and second level page tables. Equal number of bits should also be used for the first and second level page number fields. The size of each page table entry is 4 bytes.

(A) Give a diagram showing how a virtual address would be translated to a physical address.
(B) What is the number of page table entries that can be contained in each page?
(C) How many bits are available for storing protection and other information in each page table entry?

State whether the following statement is TRUE or FALSE:

It is possible to construct a binary tree uniquely whose pre-order and post-order traversals are given.

State whether the following statement is TRUE or FALSE:

If the number of leaves in a tree is not a power of 2, then the tree is not a binary tree.

A size-balanced binary tree is a binary tree in which for every node, the difference between the number of nodes in the left and right subtree is at most 1. The distance of a node from the root is the length of the path from the root to the node. The height of a binary tree is the maximum distance of a leaf from the root.

A. Prove, using induction on h, that a size-balanced binary tree of height h contains at least 2^h nodes.

B. In a size-balanced binary tree of height h >= 1, how many nodes are at distance h - 1 from the root? Write only the answer without the explanations.

Insert the characters of the string K R P C S N Y T J M into a hash table of size 10. Use the hash function

h(x) = (ord(x) - ord('a') + 1) mod 10

A. Which insertions cause collisions?
B. Display the final hash table.

options.text: Both EXTRACT-MAX(A) and INSERT(A, key) run in O(1).
EXTRACT-MAX(A) runs in O(1) whereas INSERT(A, key) runs in O(log m).
EXTRACT-MAX(A) runs in O(log m) whereas INSERT(A, key) runs in O(m).
EXTRACT-MAX(A) runs in O(log m) whereas INSERT(A, key) runs in O(log m).

stem:
Let A be a priority queue for maintaining a set of elements. Suppose A is implemented using a max-heap data structure. The operation EXTRACT-MAX(A) extracts and deletes the maximum element from A. The operation INSERT(A, key) inserts a new element in A. The properties of a max-heap are preserved at the end of each of these operations.

When A contains m elements, which one of the following statements about the worst case running time of these two operations is TRUE?

Suggest a data structure for representing a subset S of integers from 1 to n. Following operations on the set S are to be performed in constant time (independent of cardinality of S):

i.   MEMBER(X): Check whether X is in the set S or not
ii.  FIND-ONE(S): If S is not empty, return one element of S (any arbitrary element will do)
iii. ADD(X): Add integer X to set S
iv.  DELETE(S, X): Delete integer X from set S

Give pictorial examples of your data structure. Give routines in an English like language. You may assume the data structure has been suitably initialized. Clearly state your assumptions regarding initialization.

options.text: ENQUEUE = PUSH; DEQUEUE = REVERSE, POP, REVERSE
ENQUEUE = REVERSE, PUSH, REVERSE; DEQUEUE = POP

stem:
Suppose a stack implementation supports, in addition to PUSH and POP, an operation REVERSE, which reverses the order of elements on the stack.

Which of the following correctly describes how ENQUEUE and DEQUEUE are implemented using this stack with REVERSE?

List the invariant assertions at points A, B, C, D and E in program given below:

Program division (input, output)
  Const dividend = 81; divisor = 9;
  Var remainder, quotient : integer
begin
  (* (dividend >= 0) AND (divisor > 0) *)
  remainder := dividend;
  quotient := 0;
  (*A*)
  While (remainder >= divisor) do
  begin
    (*B*)
    quotient := quotient + 1;
    remainder := remainder - divisor;
    (*C*)
  end
  (*D*)
end.

What is the loop invariant at point A (before the while loop starts)?

Below figure is the flow-chart corresponding to a program to calculate the gcd of two integers, M and N respectively (M, N > 0). Use assertions at the cut points C1, C2 and C3 to prove that the flow-chart is correct.

The flow-chart implements the following logic:
  Read M, N
  x := M; y := N
  [C1]
  while x != y do
    if x > y then x := x - y
    else y := y - x
  [C2]
  write x (which equals gcd(M,N))
  [C3]

What is the invariant assertion at cut point C1 (just after initializing x=M, y=N)?
